---
title: Datasets
---

`q.dataset` assembles model inputs, targets, sample weights, and row metadata
under one canonical pandas index. It is the object that flows from feature and
label research into fitted transformations and models; it is not another data
acquisition stage.

This singular namespace is distinct from `q.data.datasets`, which contains
bundled sample market data.

## Build an aligned dataset

```python
dataset = q.dataset.build(
    features=feature_frame,
    labels=labels,
    sample_weights=weights,
    metadata=event_metadata,
)
```

Splits are optional. Construction rejects mismatched or duplicate indexes
instead of silently reindexing components, so features, labels, weights, and
event metadata cannot drift apart.

## Fixed temporal partitions

```python
dataset = q.dataset.TemporalSplit(
    train_end="2022-12-31",
    validation_end="2023-12-31",
    test_end="2024-12-31",
).apply(dataset)

model.fit(
    dataset.train.X,
    dataset.train.y,
    sample_weight=dataset.train.sample_weight,
)
predictions = model.predict(dataset.test.X)
```

Rows outside the requested boundaries receive the explicit `excluded` role,
preserving complete row membership without exposing those rows to fitting or
evaluation.

## Expanding and rolling folds

`TimeSeriesSplit` wraps
[`sklearn.model_selection.TimeSeriesSplit`](https://scikit-learn.org/stable/modules/generated/sklearn.model_selection.TimeSeriesSplit.html).
Its `n_splits`, `test_size`, `gap`, and `max_train_size` parameters retain
scikit-learn semantics. Omit `max_train_size` for expanding windows or set it
for rolling windows.

```python
expanding = q.dataset.TimeSeriesSplit(
    n_splits=5,
    test_size=63,
    gap=5,
).apply(dataset)

rolling = q.dataset.TimeSeriesSplit(
    n_splits=5,
    test_size=63,
    gap=5,
    max_train_size=504,
    name="rolling",
).apply(expanding)
```

## Purging and embargo

`PurgedTimeSeriesSplit` starts with the sklearn folds and removes fit rows whose
label horizon reaches the test partition. An integer embargo counts rows; on a
session-indexed dataset those rows are trading sessions. Timedelta strings such
as `"5D"` provide calendar-time embargoes.

```python
purged = q.dataset.PurgedTimeSeriesSplit(
    n_splits=5,
    test_size=63,
    label_end="label_end_time",
    embargo=5,
).apply(dataset)

q.dataset.split_diagnostics(purged, "purged_walk_forward", "fold_1")
q.dataset.audit_splits(purged, "purged_walk_forward")
```

## Leakage-aware transformations

`q.transform.Pipeline` delegates estimator composition to sklearn and reads QRT
partition roles. It fits only from role-`fit` rows, transforms the complete
feature frame, and preserves targets, weights, metadata, and split schemes.

```python
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

pipeline = q.transform.Pipeline(
    [
        ("impute", SimpleImputer(strategy="median")),
        ("scale", StandardScaler()),
    ]
)
processed = pipeline.fit_transform(purged, scheme="purged_walk_forward")
```

Fitting records the source scheme, fold, partitions, row count, and boundaries
in `pipeline.fit_provenance_`.

## Persistence

Datasets and all attached split metadata can be persisted with joblib:

```python
purged.save("research-dataset.joblib")
restored = q.dataset.Dataset.load("research-dataset.joblib")
```

Only load trusted joblib files because the format uses pickle internally.

See the executable [dataset splitting notebook](splitting.ipynb) for a complete
comparison of fixed, expanding, rolling, and purged splits on bundled SPY data,
including diagnostics, leakage audits, transformations, and persistence.
